sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: PSEG declares $0.67 quarterly dividend for second quarter By Investing.com
feed.summary: Public Service Enterprise Group (PSEG) has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.67 per share for the second quarter of 2026, payable by June 30, 2026. This marks PSEG's 14th consecutive year of dividend increases, with a current yield of 3.32% and a history of 56 consecutive years of dividend payments. The announcement comes alongside mixed analyst ratings following the company's recent earnings report.

feed.title: La-Z-Boy sells American Drew and Kincaid casegoods businesses By Investing.com
feed.summary: La-Z-Boy Incorporated announced the sale of its American Drew and Kincaid wholesale casegoods businesses to Banner House to focus on its core North American upholstery business. The transaction, expected to close in mid-May, supports La-Z-Boy's Century Vision strategy and allows for enhanced product selection in its stores. La-Z-Boy, which has a "GOOD" InvestingPro Financial Health score and has consistently raised its dividend for five years, will continue to offer casegoods products in its owned locations.

feed.title: Engineer sues Lockheed Martin over yanked remote work accommodation
feed.summary: A Lockheed Martin engineer, Erin Lynn Jeffreys, has filed a federal lawsuit against the company, alleging that her long-standing remote work accommodation was revoked, her hours were cut, and she faced retaliation after questioning the change. Jeffreys, who has scoliosis and chronic pain, claims the company denied her formal request for accommodation despite a decade of approved remote work, and then reduced her work schedule by 30% while four non-disabled colleagues on the same program remained fully remote. She is seeking reinstatement of her full-time remote work, lost pay, and damages.

feed.title: LAKELAND CLASS ACTION DEADLINE FRIDAY: Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C. Urges Lakeland Industries, Inc. Investors to Contact the Firm Before April 24th Regarding Their Rights
feed.summary: Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C. has announced a class action lawsuit against Lakeland Industries, Inc. (NASDAQ: LAKE) for alleged false and misleading statements issued between December 1, 2023, and December 9, 2025. Investors are encouraged to contact the law firm by April 24, 2026, to discuss their rights and apply to be appointed as lead plaintiff. The lawsuit claims that Lakeland overstated the positive impact of its Pacific Helmets and Jolly businesses, concealed significant operational issues, and that its financial guidance was unreliable, leading to a substantial stock price drop.

feed.title: EQT cuts net debt to $5.7B, wins Fitch upgrade to BBB
feed.summary: EQT Corporation reported strong first quarter 2026 results, achieving a record $1.832 billion in free cash flow and significantly reducing its net debt to $5.7 billion, nearing its $5 billion target. This strong financial performance and deleveraging led to an upgrade to BBB from Fitch Ratings. The company exceeded guidance for sales volume and kept capital expenditures below the low end of guidance, while also providing a positive outlook for Q2 2026.

feed.title: TransDigm Group Inc. stock underperforms Tuesday when compared to competitors
feed.summary: TransDigm Group Inc. (TDG) experienced a significant drop of 5.41% on Tuesday, closing at $1,205.67. This underperformance occurred during a generally negative trading session for the stock market, with both the S&P 500 Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average also declining. The stock's fall ended a two-day winning streak.

feed.title: Booking Holdings finance chief speaks in London; webcast open May 5
feed.summary: Booking Holdings (NASDAQ: BKNG) announced that its CFO, Ewout Steenbergen, will participate in a fireside chat at the Barclays 18th Annual Americas Select Conference in London on May 5, 2026, at 10:45 am CET. A live audio webcast will be available to the public on the company’s investor events page, with a replay accessible approximately 24 hours later. The announcement notes that similar event communications historically have had a modest impact on the stock price, while scheduling earnings webcasts has led to small positive reactions.

feed.title: Northrop Grumman Corp. stock underperforms Tuesday when compared to competitors
feed.summary: Northrop Grumman Corp. (NOC) stock fell by 6.98% to $611.13 on Tuesday, marking its sixth consecutive day of losses. This underperformance occurred during a generally poor trading session where the S&P 500 Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average also saw declines.

feed.title: Huntington pushes deeper into Texas with commercial banking in Austin
feed.summary: Huntington National Bank is expanding its commercial banking operations into Austin and central Texas, adding middle-market and corporate banking capabilities. This move follows the acquisitions of Houston-based Cadence Bank and Dallas-based Veritex Bank, making Huntington the eighth largest bank by deposits in Texas. The company has hired Claire Harrison as senior managing director to lead Austin's commercial efforts, and 10 Cadence branches in the region will convert to Huntington branches in mid-2026.

feed.title: Capital One (NYSE:COF) Misses Q1 CY2026 Sales Expectations
feed.summary: Capital One (NYSE:COF) missed Q1 CY2026 revenue expectations with sales of $15.23 billion, despite a 52.3% year-on-year increase. The financial services company also fell short on non-GAAP profit per share, net interest margin, efficiency ratio, and tangible book value per share estimates. Following the results, Capital One's stock traded down 4.9% to $193.00.

feed.title: Is Baxter (BAX) Quietly Reframing Its Competitive Edge Around OR Efficiency And Supply Chain Stability?
feed.summary: Baxter International (BAX) is refocusing its competitive edge on operating room efficiency and supply chain stability by introducing new surgical equipment and patient support systems. The reopening of the Strait of Hormuz could ease logistics and energy costs, potentially improving Baxter's margins despite existing risks related to product usage and cost reduction efforts. The company's investment narrative projects significant revenue and earnings growth by 2028, with a fair value indicating a potential upside based on these strategic shifts.

feed.title: Eastman Kodak is distributing all its films again. What happens next?
feed.summary: Eastman Kodak has taken back internal distribution of its stills films, after outsourcing it to Kodak Alaris since 2012. This shift has led to new product names like Ektapan and Ektacolor Pro, replacing T-Max and Portra which Kodak Alaris retains rights to. The article discusses potential implications for film pricing and whether Eastman Kodak might invest in new film development or revive discontinued stocks, facing a more competitive market than in 2012.

feed.title: Travelers companies EVP Klenk sells $1.96m in stock By Investing.com
feed.summary: Jeffrey P. Klenk, EVP and President of Bond & Specialty Insurance at Travelers Companies (NYSE:TRV), sold 6,413.654 shares of common stock for $1,956,549 on April 20, 2026. This transaction leaves Mr. Klenk with 6,075.303 shares and was reported to the SEC on April 21, 2026. The company has a history of dividend increases and recently reported strong Q1 2025 earnings, leading to varied analyst price target adjustments.

feed.title: Eastman Kodak commemorates breakthroughs shaping imaging and manufacturing industries
feed.summary: Eastman Kodak is celebrating World Creativity and Innovation Day by highlighting its historical and ongoing contributions to imaging, material science, and manufacturing. The company emphasizes its legacy of innovation and commitment to future advancements. Recent diversification includes domestic production of pharmaceutical materials and global distribution of SONORA printing plates.

feed.title: Starbucks wins as court vacates NLRB unfair labor practice ruling
feed.summary: A federal appeals court has sided with Starbucks, ruling that the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) used an incorrect standard when penalizing the company for subpoenaing pro-union workers. The Fifth Circuit Court of Appeals vacated an NLRB order that found Starbucks committed an unfair labor practice by seeking information on union activities, forcing the Board to re-evaluate the case under a "totality of the circumstances" standard. This decision means the Board must use a more context-driven approach to determine if Starbucks' actions were coercive, rather than the previous discovery-related standard.

feed.title: Lifezone Metals Limited (LZM) Stock Price Today & Analysis
feed.summary: This article provides an analysis of Lifezone Metals Limited (LZM) stock, including its current price, fundamental financials, and company overview. It highlights LZM's focus on clean energy transition through Hydromet Technology and its operations in metals extraction and refining, primarily generating revenue from Australia. The article also includes FAQs on how to buy LZM stock via the Gotrade app, minimum investment, dividend policy, and safety of trading on Gotrade.

feed.title: Incyte president, R&D head sells $1.8m in stock, acquires $1.2m in shares
feed.summary: Pablo J. Cagnoni, President, Global Head of Research and Development at Incyte Corp, recently engaged in substantial stock transactions, selling over $1.8 million in common stock while acquiring approximately $1.2 million in shares through employee stock options. These transactions occurred as Incyte shares were valued at $96.25, reflecting a 70% return over the past year, and the company is deemed undervalued by InvestingPro analysis. Incyte's financial health is rated "GREAT" with more cash than debt, as the company prepares for upcoming clinical trial results and board changes.

feed.title: Johnson & Johnson executives join RBC healthcare event on May 19
feed.summary: Johnson & Johnson (NYSE: JNJ) announced its participation in the 2026 RBC Capital Markets Global Healthcare Conference on May 19th. Management will host a Fireside Chat at 11:30 a.m. Eastern Time, which will be available via a live audio webcast on the company's investor relations website, with a replay accessible approximately 48 hours later.

feed.title: Director at Bank of New York Mellon (NYSE: BK) receives 1,592 deferred units
feed.summary: Alfred W. Zollar, a director at Bank of New York Mellon (NYSE: BK), has received a grant of 1,592 Deferred Stock Units. These units convert 1-for-1 into common shares upon vesting, which occurs at the earlier of the company's 2027 annual shareholder meeting or one year from the grant date. Following this transaction, Zollar directly holds 31,473.32 deferred stock units.

feed.title: Prudential Financial (NYSE: PRU) extends Japan new-sales suspension by 180 days
feed.summary: Prudential Financial announced an additional 180-day extension of the voluntary suspension of new sales at its subsidiary, Prudential of Japan (POJ). This extension follows an initial 90-day suspension and is due to the greater-than-expected complexity of operational, governance, and sales-conduct reforms needed. Existing policies and policyholders are not affected, and Prudential Financial reiterates that POJ remains financially sound and Japan is a core part of its global operations.

feed.title: NFL, U.S. Bank Announce Partnership
feed.summary: The National Football League and U.S. Bank have formed a multi-year partnership, making U.S. Bank the NFL's official bank and wealth-management sponsor. This collaboration includes U.S. Bank becoming the presenting sponsor of the Super Bowl MVP Award and a top-tier sponsor of the NFL FLAG Championships. Additionally, U.S. Bank's Financial Edge program will provide financial guidance to athletes, with Fernando Mendoza serving as "chief financial playmaker."

feed.title: La-Z-Boy Incorporated Announces Sale of American Drew and Kincaid Wholesale Casegoods Businesses
feed.summary: La-Z-Boy Incorporated announced the sale of its American Drew and Kincaid wholesale casegoods businesses to Banner House. This transaction allows La-Z-Boy to focus on its core vertically integrated North American upholstery business and its Century Vision strategy. The sale is expected to close in mid-May and ensures a seamless transition for employees, customers, and suppliers of the American Drew and Kincaid brands.

feed.title: Fifth Third Bancorp re-elects 16 directors at annual meeting
feed.summary: Fifth Third Bancorp re-elected all 16 directors to its board during its annual shareholders meeting, with Timothy N. Spence continuing as chairman, CEO, and president. The board comprises a diverse group of executives from various major corporations. Shareholders also approved Deloitte & Touche LLP as the independent external audit firm for 2026 and endorsed the company's executive compensation package.

feed.title: Pomerantz Law Firm Investigates Claims On Behalf of Investors of CarMax, Inc. - KMX
feed.summary: Pomerantz LLP is investigating potential securities fraud claims against CarMax, Inc. (NYSE: KMX) following a significant 15.12% drop in its stock price on April 14, 2026. This decline occurred after the company's earnings call, where management announced plans to lower used car prices and increase lending to customers with lower credit scores to address an affordability crisis in the auto industry. Investors are urged to contact the law firm for information on joining a potential class action lawsuit.

feed.title: Jabil (NYSE: JBL) COO Priestley sells 3,169 shares at $330 in open market
feed.summary: Jabil Inc.'s EVP and Chief Operations Officer, Andrew Priestley, sold 3,169 shares of the company's common stock in an open-market transaction at $330.00 per share. This sale, totaling $1,045,770, leaves Priestley with directly held 57,098 shares, indicating a routine liquidity event rather than a significant change in his economic exposure to Jabil. The transaction was reported in a Form 4 SEC filing, which also confirmed no derivative positions were listed.

feed.title: Dover unit taps Italian specialist for ultra-compact heat exchangers
feed.summary: Dover's SWEP announced a strategic partnership with Microchannel Devices (MCD) to expand its heat transfer portfolio by adding printed circuit heat exchangers (PCHEs) to its existing brazed plate heat exchangers (BPHEs). This buy-resell agreement gives SWEP access to MCD's patented diffusion-bonded PCHEs for ultra-compact, high-pressure, and extreme-temperature solutions in areas like hydrogen infrastructure, energy storage, and CO2 applications, while leveraging SWEP's global sales channels. The collaboration aims to strengthen SWEP's market position and enhance its ability to serve customers with expanded energy-efficient solutions.

feed.title: Otis lifts dividend 5%, extending payout growth since 2020 spin
feed.summary: Otis Worldwide Corporation (NYSE: OTIS) announced a 5% increase in its quarterly dividend, raising it to $0.44 per share, payable on June 12, 2026. This marks a 120% increase in the dividend since the company's spin-off in 2020, driven by the strong cash flows from its Service business. Management emphasized this decision reflects a disciplined approach to capital allocation and a commitment to delivering attractive returns to shareholders.

feed.title: La-Z-Boy sheds two furniture brands to sharpen upholstery focus
feed.summary: La-Z-Boy (NYSE: LZB) announced the sale of its American Drew and Kincaid wholesale casegoods businesses to Banner House, with the transaction expected to close in mid-May 2026. This move allows La-Z-Boy to refocus on its vertically integrated North American upholstery business and advance its Century Vision strategy, while still offering casegoods in its 374 stores. The company anticipates a seamless transition for employees, dealers, and suppliers.

feed.title: Johnson Controls plans June 1 investor site tour across 3 stops
feed.summary: Johnson Controls (NYSE: JCI) has announced its Second Quarter Fiscal 2026 earnings conference call for May 6, 2026, which will feature a live webcast. Additionally, the company will host an invitation-only "Going to Gemba Day" for investors and analysts on June 1, 2026, including site visits to JADEC in Pennsylvania, the Airside Center of Excellence manufacturing facility, and a Baltimore market office, with select portions webcast live. These events aim to provide insights into the company's proprietary Business System and operational strategies.

feed.title: Intuitive Surgical Inc Stock (ISRG) Closed Down by 3.00% on Apr 21: A Full Analysis
feed.summary: Intuitive Surgical Inc (ISRG) stock experienced a 3.00% decline on April 21, 2026, driven by several factors including analyst price target reductions, regulatory concerns over FDA alerts and recalls on stapling devices linked to serious injuries and a death, and the company's own guidance projecting slower procedure growth and tariff impacts. These pressures, combined with an evolving competitive landscape, contributed to investor caution ahead of its first-quarter earnings report.

feed.title: ConocoPhillips Stock (COP) Closed Up by 3.44% on Apr 21: A Full Analysis
feed.summary: ConocoPhillips (COP) stock closed up by 3.44% on April 21, driven by positive analyst upgrades and rising global oil prices due to geopolitical tensions. While the company recently missed revenue and EPS estimates and saw some insider selling, the "Strong Buy" Zacks Rank and favorable macroeconomic environment for oil are key factors in its current performance. Technical analysis shows neutral to oversold conditions, while fundamental data indicates strong annual revenue and net profit within the Energy - Fossil Fuels sector.

feed.title: Travelers (NYSE: TRV) legal chief gifts 2,125 company shares
feed.summary: Avrohom J. Kess, Vice Chairman and Chief Legal Officer of Travelers (NYSE: TRV), reported a bona fide gift of 2,125 shares of the company's common stock. This transaction is a non-market transfer without sales proceeds, and after the gift, Kess directly holds 48,737.422 shares of Travelers common stock. The Form 4 filing indicates this was a gift (transaction code 'G'), not a sale, and involved only non-derivative securities.

feed.title: Travelers (NYSE: TRV) EVP Klenk sells 6,414 shares in open-market trade
feed.summary: Jeffrey P. Klenk, EVP & President, Bond & Specialty Insurance at Travelers Companies (NYSE: TRV), sold 6,413.654 shares of common stock at $305.06 per share on April 20, 2026. This open-market transaction reduced his direct holdings to 6,075.303 shares. The sale is categorized as a "S" transaction code on a Form 4 SEC filing, indicating a public market sale.

feed.title: W. R. Berkley (WRB) Q1 2026 earnings jump with 21.2% ROE
feed.summary: W. R. Berkley Corporation reported a strong first quarter 2026 with net income increasing 23.4% to $515.2 million and operating income rising 22.5% to a record $514.3 million. The company achieved a 21.2% return on equity and returned $336.1 million to shareholders through share repurchases and dividends. This performance was driven by robust underwriting, with a consolidated GAAP combined ratio of 90.7%, and record net investment income of $404.3 million.

feed.title: United Airlines sees weak Q2, full-year profit as fuel shock squeezes margins
feed.summary: United Airlines has issued a disappointing profit forecast for the second quarter and the full year, citing higher jet fuel prices due to the Iran war. The airline expects to recover only 40-50% of the fuel cost increase in Q2, though premium travel demand remains robust. This outlook highlights a broader trend among U.S. carriers facing similar cost pressures.

feed.title: Self-Storage REITs Schedule Conference Calls for 1Q Results
feed.summary: The four largest publicly traded U.S. self-storage REITs—CubeSmart, Extra Space Storage Inc., National Storage Affiliates Trust, and Public Storage Inc.—have announced release dates for their first-quarter 2026 earnings. CubeSmart, Extra Space Storage, and Public Storage will host conference calls to discuss their results, while National Storage Affiliates Trust will not, instead posting supplemental materials online. These announcements detail when and how investors can access financial results and participate in or listen to the earnings calls.

feed.title: Devon Energy (NYSE: DVN) reshapes leadership pay and governance details
feed.summary: Devon Energy has filed an amendment to its annual report (Form 10-K/A) to include comprehensive disclosures on its directors, executive officers, corporate governance, and executive compensation for 2025. The company details leadership changes, including Clay Gaspar's appointment as President and CEO, and outlines a pay-for-performance program with compensation heavily weighted towards annual incentives and long-term equity tied to financial, operational, and environmental goals. The report also addresses stockholder feedback by enhancing disclosure on goal setting, adjusting the 2026 company performance scorecard design, and increasing the CEO's performance-based equity component.

feed.title: W. R. Berkley Corporation Reports First Quarter 2026 Results
feed.summary: W. R. Berkley Corporation announced strong first-quarter 2026 results, achieving an annualized 21.2% return on equity. The company reported significant growth in net income and operating income, reaching $515.2 million and a record $514.3 million, respectively. This performance was driven by increased underwriting and investment income, alongside strategic share repurchases and consistent dividend payments to shareholders.

feed.title: Monarch Casino: Q1 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Monarch Casino & Resort Inc. (MCRI) reported its first-quarter earnings, reaching $27.6 million. The Reno, Nevada-based casino operator announced a profit of $1.52 per share and revenue totaling $136.6 million for the period. This financial summary was generated using data from Zacks Investment Research.

feed.title: [8-K] INTUITIVE SURGICAL INC Reports Material Event
feed.summary: Intuitive Surgical Inc. (ISRG) reported strong first-quarter 2026 results, with revenue reaching $2.77 billion, a 23% increase year-over-year, driven by double-digit growth in robotic procedures and system placements. The company's da Vinci and Ion systems saw significant procedure growth, and GAAP net income attributable to Intuitive Surgical, Inc. was $822 million. For the full year 2026, Intuitive expects worldwide da Vinci procedure growth of 13.5%–15.5%, a non-GAAP gross margin of 67.5%–68.5%, and non-GAAP operating expense growth of 11%–14%.

feed.title: Merck And Eisai’s Keytruda/Lenvima/Welireg Trial In Kidney Cancer Fails
feed.summary: Merck and Eisai announced that their LITESPARK-012 trial, investigating a combination of Keytruda, Lenvima, and Welireg for kidney cancer, failed to meet its primary endpoints. This news was reported by Scrip on April 21, 2026. This setback affects the clinical development of their integrated oncology strategy.

feed.title: Rithm Property Trust sets May payouts for common and preferred shares
feed.summary: Rithm Property Trust Inc. (NYSE: RPT) announced its Board of Directors has declared first-quarter 2026 common and preferred stock dividends. A cash dividend of $0.36 per share of common stock will be payable on May 29, 2026, to stockholders of record as of May 15, 2026. Additionally, a Series C preferred stock dividend of $0.6171875 per share is payable on May 15, 2026, to preferred stockholders of record on May 1, 2026.

feed.title: INCYTE CORP (INCY) executive exercises options and sells 18,667 shares
feed.summary: INCYTE CORP's President and Global Head of R&D, Pablo J. Cagnoni, executed an option exercise and subsequent sale of 18,667 shares on April 17, 2026. He acquired shares at strike prices of $71.93 and $64.25 and sold them at an average price of $96.50 per share. Following these transactions, Cagnoni still directly holds 262,692 shares, including a significant portion of unvested restricted and performance stock units.

feed.title: Public Service Enterprise Group Keeps Quarterly Dividend at $0.67 a Share, Payable June 30 to Holders of Record June 9
feed.summary: Public Service Enterprise Group (PSEG) has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.67 per share. The dividend is payable on June 30 to shareholders of record as of June 9. PSEG operates primarily in electricity and natural gas distribution, electricity transmission, and power generation.

feed.title: Council committee advances $12 million in city incentives for Johnson & Johnson expansion
feed.summary: A City Council Finance Committee has approved $12 million in city incentives for Johnson & Johnson Vision Care. This funding will support the construction of a new packaging distribution facility in Northwest Jacksonville and the installation of high-tech equipment at its Southside campus, with a total investment by J&J of $550 million. The incentives are structured as two Recapture Enhanced Value Grants, conditional on maintaining a specific number of jobs.

feed.title: Prudential keeps Japan life insurance sales frozen for 6 more months
feed.summary: Prudential Financial, Inc. (PRU) has extended the voluntary suspension of new sales activity at its Japanese subsidiary, Prudential of Japan (POJ), by an additional 180 days. This decision was made because the necessary operational, governance, and organizational changes are more complex and extensive than initially anticipated. The suspension aims to allow POJ to implement reforms across key areas to prevent misconduct, strengthen accountability, and transform its sales model, ensuring long-term customer outcomes while maintaining financial soundness and servicing existing policies.

feed.title: Paycom to post March-quarter results after the close on May 6
feed.summary: Paycom Software, Inc. (NYSE: PAYC) announced it will release its first-quarter 2026 financial results after the market closes on May 6. The company will also host a conference call at 5 p.m. Eastern on the same day to discuss the results. Interested parties can access the call via a dial-in number or through a webcast available on Paycom's investor relations website.

feed.title: For 225,000 Iowa customers, Earth Day starts with fixing leaks
feed.summary: Iowa American Water marked Earth Day by emphasizing water conservation, infrastructure stewardship, and community engagement. The utility, serving approximately 225,000 customers, encouraged simple household actions like fixing leaks and proper disposal of medications to protect water resources. Additionally, they highlighted National Prescription Drug Take Back Day for safe medication disposal.

feed.title: March 2026 card and auto losses at Capital One (NYSE: COF)
feed.summary: Capital One Financial Corporation (NYSE: COF) released its monthly credit quality metrics for March 2026, detailing performance in its domestic credit card and consumer banking auto loan portfolios. The domestic credit card portfolio reported a net charge-off rate of 5.09% and a 30+ day performing delinquency rate of 3.70%. The consumer banking auto portfolio showed a net charge-off rate of 1.44% and a 30+ day performing delinquency rate of 4.21%.

feed.title: Halliburton CEO: U.S. oil is in the ‘early innings’ of a rebound—and a drilling ramp up is coming
feed.summary: Halliburton CEO Jeff Miller states the U.S. oil sector is in the "early innings" of a rebound driven by the Iran war, which is forcing a global shift towards energy security and increased domestic production outside the Middle East. He anticipates a drilling ramp-up, especially from smaller U.S. producers, and significant growth in South America and Africa to replace lost supplies from the conflict. Halliburton reported increased first-quarter net income, showcasing resilience despite disruptions in the Middle East.
